description: Structured GET request input for API debugging, webhook checks, or file downloads. The tool returns raw HTTP response content and does not render webpages or convert HTML to Markdown.

params.description: Optional query params object. Object values are URL-encoded and appended automatically.

params_list.description: Optional raw query fragment array such as ["q=hello", "page=1"].

headers.description: Optional request headers as a simple object map.

header_lines.description: Optional raw header string array such as ["Accept: application/json"].

bearer.description: Optional bearer token shortcut. Adds Authorization: Bearer <token> unless Authorization is already provided in headers.

basic.description: Optional basic auth shortcut object with username and password.

basic_text.description: Optional basic auth shortcut string in the form user:pass.

timeout_ms.description: Optional request timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to 60000.

follow_location.description: Optional redirect-follow flag for common 30x cases.

download_to.description: Optional local file path used to save the response body. Prefer this for binary payloads or file downloads instead of inline webpage-style reading.

save_headers_to.description: Optional local file path used to save response headers.

include_headers.description: Compatibility switch that includes response headers in the rendered output. Prefer flags="response-header" for new callers.

flags.description: Optional comma-separated render flags string. Example: "request-header,response-header". Empty by default. Use request-header to include request details, response-header to include response headers. Unknown flags are ignored. Inline body output is raw HTTP content, not webpage rendering or Markdown conversion.
